messages.content: You digest raw market-research search results into citable claim atoms for a risk-hunting pass over a market model. Extract only what the text actually says — every excerpt must be verbatim. Numeric claims are worth more than qualitative ones.

THE CURATED LEDGER NODES THIS GROUP FEEDS (with their derivations — compare against them):

     "title": "Infoblatt Versorgungsmessgeräte",
     "url": "https://www.lbme.nrw.de/system/files/media/document/file/e_info_versorgungsmessgeraete_2023-03-29.pdf",
     "publishedDate": null,
     "text": "Stand: 29. März 2023

## Informationen über eichrechtliche Bestimmungen für Elektrizitäts-, Gas-, Wasser- und Wärmezähler

Messgeräte für Elektrizität, Gas, Wasser und Wärme gehören zahlenmäßig zu den größten Gruppen jener Mess geräte, auf die das Eichrecht anzuwenden ist. Durch die steigenden Kosten für Energie und Rohstoffe, sowie einer verbrauchsorientierten und somit gerechten Kostenverteilung auf die einzelnen Verbraucher, ist die korrekte Anzeige dieser Messgeräte innerhalb garantierter Fehlergrenzen von großer wirtschaftlicher Bedeutung. Seit dem Inkrafttreten des Mess- und Eichgesetzes (MessEG 1) und der Mess- und Eichverordnung (MessEV 2) zum 01.01.2015 werden neue Versorgungsmessgeräte vom Hersteller als konformitätsbewertete Messgeräte (mit MID-Kennzeichnung, siehe Rückseite) unter Mitwirkung einer vom Hersteller gewählten Konformitätsbewertungs stelle in Verkehr gebracht. Dabei müssen die Messgeräte, die der europäischen Messgeräterichtlinie 2014/32/EU (MID 3) unterliegen, die wesentlichen Anforderungen der MID erfüllen. Diese Messgeräte gelten im Sinne des MessEG als geeicht.

Bis Ende 2014 konnten Versorgungsmessgeräte überwiegend durch staatlich anerkannte Prüfstellen für Mess geräte für Elektrizität, Gas, Wasser oder Wärme erstgeeicht (amtliche Prüfung) in Verkehr gebracht werden. Hier bei handelt es sich um nationale Einrichtungen, z. B. bei Versorgungsunternehmen oder Herstellern, die im Be reich der Messgeräte für die Energie und Wasserversorgung Aufgaben d"

     "title": "Anzeigepflicht für Messgeräte nach MessEG und MessEV | GOSSEN METRAWATT",
     "url": "https://www.gossenmetrawatt.de/wissen/energiemanagement-mit-system/anzeigepflicht-fuer-messgeraete-nach-messeg-und-messev/",
     "publishedDate": null,
     "text": "Anzeigepflicht für Messgeräte nach MessEG und MessEV | GOSSEN METRAWATT

# Anzeigepflicht für Messgeräte nach MessEG und MessEV

Mit Inkrafttretens des Mess- und Eichgesetztes (MessEV) und der Mess- und Eichverordnung (MessEV) sind Messgeräteverwender verpflichtet Messgeräte, die zu Abrechnungszwecken dienen, binnen sechs Wochen an die zuständige Eichbehörde zu melden.Mit dem MessEG entfällt die bisherige Ersteichung von Messgeräten. Dadurch haben die Eichbehörden grundsätzlich keine Kenntnis mehr über den Standort verwendeter Messgeräte. Damit wie bisher eine wirksame Verwendungs- und/oder Marktüberwachung im Sinne des Verbraucherschutzes möglich ist, hat der Gesetzgeber die Anzeigepflicht eingeführt.Grundsätzlich müssen alle verwendeten neuen oder erneuerten Messgeräte im Anwendungsbereich von MessEG und MessEV angezeigt werden. Die Anzeigepflicht gilt nicht für Maßverkörperungen wie Gewichtstücke oder Ausschankmaße und nicht für Zusatzeinrichtungen.Messgeräte im Sinne von § 32 MessEG sind alle Geräte oder Systeme mit einer Messfunktion, die jeweils zur Verwendung im geschäftlichen oder amtlichen Verkehr oder zur Durchführung von Messungen im öffentlichen Interesse bestimmt sind. Wenn also z. B. mit einem geeichten MID-Zähler Strom nach abgegebener kWh verkauft wird – beispielsweise an einen Untermieter in einer Anlage - handelt es sich um ein oder mehrere Messgeräte im Sinne von § 32 MessEG.Die Anzeigepflicht betrifft alle neuen oder erneuerte Messgeräte, die ab dem 01.01."

     "title": "Eichrecht Implementation - Deep dive into German Measurement and Calibration Law - E-Mobility Simplified",
     "url": "https://www.emobilitysimplified.com/2022/03/eichrecht-implementation-deep-dive-into.html",
     "publishedDate": "2022-03-01T00:00:00.000Z",
     "text": "Eichrecht Implementation - Deep dive into German Measurement and Calibration Law - E-Mobility Simplified

Are you a beginner looking to get some basic idea of what Eichrecht is all about? Then, I would strongly advise you to first read my another post Simplified guide to Eichrecht conformity I German calibration law for electric vehicle charging. This post may further elaborate to those who have some basic idea and looking to start implementation.

## Basic of Eichrecht Calibration Law:

The Basic principle of Eichrecht is the verifiable accuracy of measured values. , ensuring that the customer pays only for what they receive.

Accordingly, the German Measurement and Calibration Law (MessEG) and the Measurement and Calibration Act (MessEV) require that the measured values be determined by a calibrated measuring device and that data records be kept in a secure and verifiable manner.

However, the law fails to offer any implementation recommendations on the procedure/ instrument to validate the data. As a result, 3 different approaches available in the market:

■ S.A.F.E initiative – a workflow that allows the customer to verify meter values independent via Transparency software

■ SAM (Storage and Display Modules) – a memory and display module installed in the charging station., where no online verification possible.

■ The ”Innogy” Solution – a complete system certification with a mobile application.

Here in this post, we shall further deep-dive into Eichrecht implementation"

     "text": "Legally Compliant Billing: Retrofitting MID Meters or Using Calibration Law-Compliant Wallboxes? Legally Compliant Billing: Retrofitting MID Meters or Using Calibration Law-Compliant Wallboxes?

# Legally Compliant Billing: Retrofitting MID Meters or Using Calibration Law-Compliant Wallboxes?Blog

Retrofit a wallbox with an MID meter or opt for a legal-for-trade wallbox? Discover the differences, advantages, and challenges of both options.

BY Maike Eggengoor / ON Jan 12, 2026

There are many rumors about the necessity of calibration law-compliant wallboxes. While some billing solution providers believe that wallboxes with MID meters are sufficient for billing company cars, others consider a so-called calibration law-compliant wallbox indispensable. This often leads to confusion among customers. This blog post aims to shed some light on the matter.

## Latest update from the metrology authorities (AGME, 09 January 2026)

Germany’s metrology authorities released additional technical guidance on home charging in early 2026. They clarified that a permanently installed MID‑compliant meter can be sufficient in clearly defined one‑to‑one scenarios. Mobile MID meters integrated into charging cables, however, do not meet the requirements and are not permitted according to AGME. A detailed assessment of these clarifications is available in the article: Home Charging for Company EVs: Metrology Authorities Clarify Compliance Requirements

### 01Conflicting systems

A cooling fault in hall 2 affects customers A, B and C simultaneously, each with their own SLA and their own escalation path. Your BMS shows the alarm. Your DCIM shows rack occupancy. Contract breach. A customer conversation you couldn't prepare for. Tenant isolation is not a configuration detail, it is an operational obligation that no update can break.

     "text": "Meter-to-Cash (M2C) in Germany: An overview | Stripe

Businesses in Germany have a wide range of options when it comes to billing for their services. In addition to flat-rate fees and subscription-based models, the so-called meter-to-cash (M2C) billing model has grown more popular recently. This increased popularity is particularly notable in the energy supply industry, as a result of a change in the law in 2023.

In this article, we'll explain what meter-to-cash is, how it works and the benefits it offers. We'll also look at the role the process plays in Germany and how businesses can offer it to their customers.

content: {"claims":[{"claim":"MessEG (Mess- und Eichgesetz) governs the placing on the market, provision, and use of measuring devices including calibration (Eichung), most recently amended by an Act of 23 October 2024","number":null,"unit":null,"sourceTitle":"Gesetz über das Inverkehrbringen und die Bereitstellung von Messgeräten auf dem Markt, ihre Verwendung und Eichung sowie über Fertigpackungen (Mess- und Eichgesetz - MessEG)","url":"https://www.gesetze-im-internet.de/messeg/MessEG.pdf","excerpt":"Mess- und Eichgesetz vom 25. Juli 2013 (BGBl. I S. 2722, 2723), das zuletzt durch Artikel 38 des Gesetzes vom 23. Oktober 2024 (BGBl. 2024 I Nr. 323) geändert worden ist","tags":["regulation","definition"]},{"claim":"Since MessEG/MessEV entered force (1 Jan 2015), new supply measuring devices are placed on the market by manufacturers as conformity-assessed devices bearing MID marking and are deemed calibrated ('geeicht') under MessEG without separate first-calibration","number":null,"unit":null,"sourceTitle":"Infoblatt Versorgungsmessgeräte","url":"https://www.lbme.nrw.de/system/files/media/document/file/e_info_versorgungsmessgeraete_2023-03-29.pdf","excerpt":"werden neue Versorgungsmessgeräte vom Hersteller als konformitätsbewertete Messgeräte (mit MID-Kennzeichnung, siehe Rückseite) unter Mitwirkung einer vom Hersteller gewählten Konformitätsbewertungsstelle in Verkehr gebracht... Diese Messgeräte gelten im Sinne des MessEG als geeicht.","tags":["regulation","definition"]},{"claim":"Users of measuring devices used for billing purposes must register them with the responsible calibration authority within six weeks","number":6,"unit":"weeks","sourceTitle":"Anzeigepflicht für Messgeräte nach MessEG und MessEV | GOSSEN METRAWATT","url":"https://www.gossenmetrawatt.de/wissen/energiemanagement-mit-system/anzeigepflicht-fuer-messgeraete-nach-messeg-und-messev/","excerpt":"sind Messgeräteverwender verpflichtet Messgeräte, die zu Abrechnungszwecken dienen, binnen sechs Wochen an die zuständige Eichbehörde zu melden.","tags":["regulation","execution"]},{"claim":"A calibrated MID meter used to sell electricity by measured kWh to a sub-tenant within a facility qualifies as a measuring device under §32 MessEG, explicitly citing sub-metering to a sub-tenant as an example","number":null,"unit":null,"sourceTitle":"Anzeigepflicht für Messgeräte nach MessEG und MessEV | GOSSEN METRAWATT","url":"https://www.gossenmetrawatt.de/wissen/energiemanagement-mit-system/anzeigepflicht-fuer-messgeraete-nach-messeg-und-messev/","excerpt":"Wenn also z. B. mit einem geeichten MID-Zähler Strom nach abgegebener kWh verkauft wird – beispielsweise an einen Untermieter in einer Anlage - handelt es sich um ein oder mehrere Messgeräte im Sinne von § 32 MessEG.","tags":["regulation","substitution","execution"]},{"claim":"MessEG/MessEV require measured values used for billing to be determined by a calibrated measuring device with secure, verifiable data records, but the law provides no implementation procedure/instrument for validation, leading to three divergent market approaches (S.A.F.E., SAM modules, full system certification)","number":3,"unit":"approaches","sourceTitle":"Eichrecht Implementation - Deep dive into German Measurement and Calibration Law - E-Mobility Simplified","url":"https://www.emobilitysimplified.com/2022/03/eichrecht-implementation-deep-dive-into.html","excerpt":"the law fails to offer any implementation recommendations on the procedure/ instrument to validate the data. As a result, 3 different approaches available in the market: S.A.F.E initiative...SAM (Storage and Display Modules)...The 'Innogy' Solution","tags":["regulation","execution"]},{"claim":"German metrology authorities (AGME) clarified in Jan 2026 that a permanently installed MID-compliant meter can be sufficient in clearly defined one-to-one billing scenarios, while mobile MID meters integrated into charging cables do not meet requirements","number":null,"unit":null,"sourceTitle":"Legally Compliant Billing: Retrofitting MID Meters or Using Calibration Law-Compliant Wallboxes?","url":"https://charge-repay.io/en/blog/mid-zaehler-nachruesten-vs-eichrechtskonforme-wallbox/","excerpt":"a permanently installed MID‑compliant meter can be sufficient in clearly defined one‑to‑one scenarios. Mobile MID meters integrated into charging cables, however, do not meet the requirements and are not permitted according to AGME.","tags":["regulation"]},{"claim":"A Frankfurt colo pricing example (Digital Realty FRA18) shows billing structured as a base power allowance (3 kW included) plus an excess-usage fee charged per 0.1 kW increment above that threshold, rather than pure metered-kWh invoicing","number":0.1,"unit":"kW increments","sourceTitle":"Full Rack Colocation in Digital Realty FRA18 Frankfurt","url":"https://www.voxility.com/colocation/prices/Full+Rack+Colocation+in+Digital+Realty+FRA18+Frankfurt","excerpt":"Each additional 0,1 kW power draw above 3 kW included is charged with an excessive usage fee of $15544/0,1 kW","tags":["execution","structure"]},{"claim":"A colo monitoring vendor describes current industry practice as energy billing based on estimates, with manual rack-level allocation and estimation where measurement points are missing","number":null,"unit":null,"sourceTitle":"Colocation Data Centre Monitoring – Vaiking","url":"https://vaiking.de/en/branchen/colocation","excerpt":"03Energy billing based on estimates. End of month: energy data export from three systems, manual allocation at rack level, estimates where measurement points are missing.","tags":["execution","base-rate"]},{"claim":"Meter-to-cash billing adoption in Germany has grown, particularly in the energy supply industry, following a change in the law in 2023","number":2023,"unit":"year","sourceTitle":"Meter-to-Cash (M2C) in Germany: An overview | Stripe","url":"https://stripe.com/en-de/resources/more/meter-to-cash-germany","excerpt":"This increased popularity is particularly notable in the energy supply industry, as a result of a change in the law in 2023.","tags":["regulation","demand"]},{"claim":"None of the retrieved Equinix, Digital Realty, or NTT Germany data-center materials specify whether tenant billing uses facility-feed/RPP MID-certified meters versus PDU-level metering","number":null,"unit":null,"sourceTitle":"Germany Data Centers, Colocation & AI-Ready Infrastructure","url":"https://www.equinix.com/data-centers/europe-colocation/germany-colocation","excerpt":"Deploy closer to customers and partners across Germany and the EU. Store and process data in secure, AI-ready data centers with direct connections to all major clouds and networks.","tags":["structure"]}],"tensions":[{"text":"The GOSSEN METRAWATT example states that any MID-calibrated meter used to sell electricity per measured kWh to a sub-tenant (e.g., in a colo facility) itself falls under §32 MessEG obligations — implying PDU-level sub-metering used for tenant billing would need to be MID-compliant, not merely 'informational' as the query's premise assumes. This complicates serviceableFactor's qualitative EU-cert-stack rationale, which treats regulatory reach as a single haircut rather than distinguishing facility-feed vs. PDU billing compliance paths.","nodes":["serviceableFactor"]},{"text":"Vaiking's disclosure that current colo industry billing is often 'based on estimates' with manual rack-level allocation directly contradicts the search intent's premise that tenant power is reliably invoiced at facility-feed/RPP MID-certified meters, suggesting the regulatory-clear billing practice assumed in serviceableFactor's derivation may not be uniformly true across German operators.","nodes":["serviceableFactor"]},{"text":"The Digital Realty/Voxility pricing model (fixed kW allowance + per-0.1kW overage fee) is a capacity/threshold-based billing structure, not a pure MID-certified metered-kWh invoice — this is a distinct billing paradigm from both the 'facility-feed MID meter' and 'PDU-as-informational' framings posed in the query, indicating the underlying regulatory/commercial reality may be more heterogeneous than either node assumption captures.","nodes":["serviceableFactor"]}],"absences":["No German colocation operator (Equinix, Digital Realty, NTT, or others) publicly discloses or confirms whether tenant power is billed via facility-feed/RPP MID-certified meters versus PDU-level metering treated as informational — the core question of the intent remains unanswered by any retrieved source.","No operator billing interviews were found despite being an explicitly targeted source type in the query design.","No MessEG/MID applicability determination specifically for data-center colocation PDU sub-metering (as opposed to EV charging or general supply metering) was found — all regulatory guidance retrieved addresses electricity/water/gas supply meters or EV charging, not colo rack-level PDU billing.","No quantification exists of what share of German colo capacity or contracts use MID-certified sub-metering versus estimate-based/capacity-tier billing, leaving the serviceableFactor regulatory haircut (0.55, range 0.45–0.65) without an empirical anchor from this metering-specific investigation.","No cost or compliance-burden estimate was found for retrofitting PDU-level meters to MID/Eichrecht standards in a colo context, which would be needed to assess whether metering compliance materially reduces serviceable demand."]}
